prompt:
Please generate a complete script that uses the Groq API to group user reviews into meaningful categories.

Here’s what the script should do:

Be saved as src/05_personas_auto.py Load reviews from data/reviews_clean.jsonl Each review has review_id and clean_content Only use the first 100 reviews to avoid token limits

Use the Groq API only (no gensim, sklearn, LDA, or embeddings), and use this model: meta-llama/llama-4-scout-17b-16e-instruct

The script should send the reviews to the model and ask it to group them into exactly 5 categories based on meaning.

Each group should include:

group_id (G1–G5) theme (short name) description (1–2 sentences) review_ids (list of review IDs)

Also make sure:

Each review appears in only one group Duplicate review IDs are removed Any ```json formatting in the response is cleaned group_id values are properly formatted as G1–G5

The final results should be saved to: data/review_groups_auto.json

Make sure the output is valid JSON.

Code should be clean, readable, and include comments and basic error handling.

Inside the script, the prompt sent to the model should clearly ask for meaningful themes (similar to common app review categories) and avoid empty groups.

Finally, just return the Python script — no explanations or markdown.
